title: Advanced Phrasal and Prepositional Verbs
description: Command the multi-word verbs that define near-native fluency: idiomatic two-part and three-part phrasal verbs (put up with, look down on, get away with, do away with), separability rules, precise meaning versus near-synonyms, and register — from slang to formal equivalents.
sources: CEFR C2 descriptors (Council of Europe, Common European Framework of Reference)
Oxford Phrasal Verbs Dictionary (Oxford University Press, 2nd edition)
